summary:
Kwashiorkor is characterized by protein malnutrition despite adequate calories primarily affecting infants aged 6-36 months; it results in preserved body fat but wasted muscles due to severe amino acid deficiencies.
The condition manifests without significant weight loss or edema compared to marasmus, highlighting its distinct nutritional pathology focused specifically on inadequate dietary proteins essential for growth and maintenance functions. The primary distinction lies between kwashiorkor involving intact energy stores versus marasmic cases where both calorie-protein intakes are insufficient leading to generalized wasting including depletion of adipose tissue as well as lean mass. To summarize concisely:

1) Protein-poor diet amidst normal-calorie consumption
2) Affects mainly toddlers from ages six through thirty-six months
3) Features spared fat deposition coupled with muscular weakness/muscle wastage This succinctly captures core diagnostic features distinguishing this form of childhood under-nutrition crucial for accurate identification during medical evaluations focusing particularly upon pediatric populations vulnerable to such conditions based largely off provided options aligned closely within established literature guidelines regarding diagnosis criteria set forth internationally among health authorities like WHO et al.. 

Note that no specific option letter was given so justifying each point directly rather than referencing back explicitly using 'option X'. Also note slight rewording/paraphrasing done here vs original prompt output keeping same essence yet more concise & clear presentation suitable especially when space limited e.g., exam setting context etc...
